description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and tapering towards the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, held tightly together by three evenly spaced metal hoops or bands that encircle the barrel horizontally. The wood has a dark, weathered appearance with visible grain and subtle stains, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood, with the top appearing to be securely closed. The metal bands are slightly tarnished, adding to the rustic, utilitarian look. There are no visible handles, spouts, or markings, making this barrel suitable for general storage or decorative use in urban environments.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with three metal hoops, bulging sides, and a closed flat top. The wood is dark and aged, with no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full cylindrical shape, three metal hoops, and the flat, closed wooden top.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the curvature of the staves and the weathered texture of the wood.
description_view_2: Another side view with similar features, emphasizing the symmetry and even spacing of the metal bands.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the same structural details and the consistent finish of the wood and metal.
